active_blocker: A733 SDMMC0 now proves card init, pre-mmcblk CMD17, small PIO CMD18 reads, partition discovery, CMD49 cache-enable via a scoped PIO write, and read-only EXT4 root mount to init=/bin/sh when IDMA is bypassed. H009 proves that even a single 8-block, 4096-byte CMD18 IDMA read stalls before partition parsing. H010 closes descriptor geometry. H012 closes DMAC bit 0x200 as a mainline-only clue. H013 clears the PIO rail's leftover ACCESS_BY_AHB before IDMA and still stalls. H014 identifies vendor GCTRL bit 0x00000800 as SDXC_DTIME_UNIT, a read-data-timeout scaling bit. H015 closes the cheap hidden DMA/fabric source audit. H016 proves the coherent descriptor ring is unchanged after the stall. H017 found no remaining source-backed IOMMU, descriptor geometry, DMA-mask, or SDMMC0 fabric-clock delta that justifies another visible-control build. H018 removed the forced A733 64-bit DMA-mask path: the data buffer moved below 4 GiB and the stall remained unchanged, but the coherent descriptor ring stayed at 0xfe100000. H019 moved the descriptor ring to streaming-mapped normal memory at 0xfa800000 and the data buffer to 0xfa802000; the stall remained unchanged. The current blocker is below descriptor memory class: SDMMC0 IDMAC master/fabric reachability, address translation, security/firewall, or an unobserved non-SDMMC control path, not rootfs, card protocol, descriptor geometry, data-buffer >4G placement, descriptor allocation class, visible GCTRL/DMAC bits, or board DTS scope.

operator_model.restore_after_test: Power-cycle Cubie3 and verify vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733 before ending the session.

session_exit_gates.hypothesis_queue_update_committed: Update and commit this file. Sync it to /srv/projects/homelab/task-packets/kernel/a733-hypothesis-queue.json on Strix.
session_exit_gates.workflow_automation_recorded: If the upstream preparation process changed, update the public b4, validation-floor, topic-branch, PR-template, and review-export docs before ending the session.
session_exit_gates.remote_push_checked: Push repos with configured remotes and record any repo that has no remote.

guardrails: Do not add vendor U-Boot compatibility strings, aliases, paths, or policy nodes to upstream DTS.
Do not expand into Ethernet, VPU, display, USB-C, PCIe, or wireless.
Do not submit local A733 CCU or pinctrl scaffolding while Junhui Liu's CCU/PRCM RFC and Andre Przywara's pinctrl RFC are in flight.
Keep clk_ignore_unused and PIO bypasses as lab diagnostics only.

submission_workflow.series_manager: Use b4 from a clean generated review branch for final mailing-list preparation.
submission_workflow.branch_hierarchy: Use topic branches, candidate branches, and generated review branches; update dependent refs together with git rebase --update-refs when rebasing a stack.
submission_workflow.validation_floor: Run scripts/kernel-validation-floor locally; the public repo CI mirrors this floor for candidate/**, topic/**, review/**, codex/**, pull requests, and manual dispatch.
submission_workflow.review_export: Treat patches/ as a review snapshot only. Regenerate it from the final branch instead of editing flat patch files by hand.
submission_workflow.stop_conditions: Use the public PR template checklist before human review or any b4 send --reflect step.

latest_completed_test.title: Streaming-mapped descriptor ring outside coherent CMA
latest_completed_test.result: Moving the descriptor ring from coherent CMA allocation to streaming-mapped normal memory did not fix descriptor fetch. The descriptor ring moved to sg_dma=0xfa800000/DLBA=0x3ea00000 and the data buffer moved to sg0 dma=0xfa802000. Descriptor checksum and stamps remained unchanged after cpu sync, OWN stayed set, CHDA stayed at DLBA, CBDA stayed zero, and IDST stayed 0x4000. H019 closes descriptor allocation class as the immediate cause.

hypotheses.title: Fixed vendor-vs-mainline data-phase register comparison before another boot variant
hypotheses.question: Which visible SDMMC0 register differs at the same data-phase moment where vendor advances CHDA/CBDA and mainline remains in descriptor-read state?
hypotheses.test_cost: low; source/log analysis first, no kernel build required
procedure: Read the vendor SDMMC0 wide register sample and the latest mainline UART log.
Fill task-packets/kernel/a733-sdmmc-register-dump-template.json for the phases vendor-working-read, mainline-dma-entry, mainline-idma-post-cmdr, and mainline-stall-poll.

Compare GCTRL, CLKCR, WIDTH, CMDR, CARG, IMASK, RINTR, STAS, NTSR, FTRGL, THLD, DMAC, DLBA, IDST, IDIE, CHDA, CBDA, BLKSZ, BCNTR, CBCR, BBCR, FUNS, DBGC, CSDC, DRV_DL, SAMP_DL, DS_DL, SKEW_CTRL, and SMCV.
If a single non-cosmetic delta remains, create the next minimal patch from that delta only. If no visible delta remains, move to H002.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A register delta is identified that plausibly controls data/DMA progress and is absent from prior falsified tests.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No visible register delta remains; next work must inspect hidden wrapper/source behavior instead of cycling another visible-register boot variant.

result.summary: Most visible setup registers match: CMDR, FTRGL, THLD, DMAC, IDIE, FUNS, DRV_DL, NTSR, SMCV. Vendor advances CHDA/CBDA and data counters, while mainline remains at CHDA=DLBA and CBDA=0. The only setup-looking visible delta is SAMP_DL=0x00002000 on vendor versus 0 on mainline, but the raw SAMP_DL write was already tested and did not stick. Do not create another visible-register boot variant without source evidence.

hypotheses.title: Find hidden v5p3x wrapper, timing-phase, or MBUS/storage behavior in vendor source
hypotheses.question: Does the vendor A733 v5p3x path program a non-SDMMC register, wrapper bit, or timing phase path required before IDMAC can fetch descriptors?
hypotheses.test_cost: low to medium; source search before build
partial_results: Orange Pi A733 v5p3x source has a direct REG_SAMP_DL programming block, but it is commented out.
The active vendor phase path writes REG_SD_NTSR and saves/restores REG_SAMP_DL; the v5p3x init path does not assign the sunxi_mmc_set_samp_dl hook.
The preserved-SAMP_DL diagnostic proves SAMP_DL=0x00002000 survives into the failing mainline CMD18, but IDMAC still sticks in descriptor-read state.
Explicit SDMMC0 consumers for mmc_store, mmc_mbus, and mmc_msi_lite were already tested and did not fix IDMA.
Vendor A733 DTS uses CLK_MSI_LITE1 for SDMMC0; the local diagnostic changed only the SDMMC0 mmc_msi_lite consumer to CLK_BUS_MSI_LITE1 and still hit the same CMD18 IDMA descriptor-read stall.
Vendor SDMMC0 DTS sets req-page-count=<2>; changing local descriptor allocation to two pages produced descriptor sync size=8192 and still hit the same CMD18 IDMA descriptor-read stall.

A source scan found no active A733 runtime wrapper write outside the SDMMC register window. The ioremap writes in the available MMC vendor code are FPGA/SUN300/CQE or commented diagnostic paths, not the Cubie A7S SDMMC0 runtime path.

Vendor ctl-spec-caps=<0x428> maps to retry, manual read-data-timeout, and CMD11 timeout handling. The vendor baseline still shows TMOUT=0xffffffff at idle, and this path is not a plausible descriptor-fetch enable.
procedure: Continue the vendor/source search outside the already-falsified direct SAMP_DL, sample-calibration, and explicit mmc_store/mmc_mbus/mmc_msi_lite paths.
Search for SDMMC0, v5p3x, IDMAC, MBUS, MSI-lite, storage fabric, wrapper register writes outside the SDMMC register window, cache/MBUS attributes, and any A733-specific IDMAC enable sequence.
Record file, function, register address, value, and call path.
Only if a concrete write is found, build a minimal diagnostic that adds that one write with printk evidence.
Boot Cubie3 once, capture UART, restore vendor kernel, and update this queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A specific hidden register/write is found and tested.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No source-backed hidden write exists in available vendor code.
hypotheses.result: No active A733/Cubie3 SDMMC0 hidden wrapper write was found in the available vendor runtime path. Source-backed subtests for SAMP_DL preservation, SDMMC0 MSI Lite1 clock mapping, and exact SDMMC0 req-page-count descriptor allocation were all falsified. Move to the lower-level CMD17 proof.

hypotheses.title: Lower-level CMD17 proof outside mmcblk queue setup
hypotheses.question: Can a single-block read complete cleanly when issued as a targeted diagnostic instead of forcing block-layer queue geometry?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small kernel patch and one boot
procedure: Do not change global mmcblk queue limits.
Add a one-shot diagnostic after card init that issues or emulates one controlled 512-byte read and logs RINTR, STAS, FIFO, and data bytes.
Boot once and capture UART.
Restore Cubie3 and update the queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: Controlled CMD17 completes and returns data, separating single-block protocol from CMD18/multi-block behavior.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: CMD17 also stops at command-done/RX-request with no data-over.

result.summary: Passed. Pre-mmcblk CMD17 completed with ret=0 and 512 bytes copied from FIFO. The data ended with 0xaa55, then mmcblk0 and partitions p1/p2/p3 appeared. Small PIO CMD18 reads of 8 blocks also completed. The remaining failure is the later 256-block IDMA CMD18.

hypotheses.title: PIO-sized block-layer rootfs proof
hypotheses.question: Can Cubie3 mount the SD rootfs if block-layer reads are constrained to the small PIO-sized request shape that H003 proved works?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small kernel patch and one boot
procedure: Keep this as a lab diagnostic only; do not present it as an upstream fix.
Patch only the A733 diagnostic path so SDMMC0 advertises small block-layer limits, preferably max_blk_count=8, max_segs=1, max_seg_size=4096, and max_req_size=4096.
Keep the existing PIO path for reads with blocks<=8 and blksz=512.
Build Image/DTB from Strix, install into Cubie3 /boot/cthu, flush storage, and boot once over UART with the same PARTUUID ro root proof.
Capture whether EXT4 mounts and /bin/sh starts, or whether any <=8-block request still stalls.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: EXT4 mounts read-only and init=/bin/sh starts, proving the card/rootfs/data path works when avoiding the large IDMA request shape.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: A small PIO-sized block request stalls, or the block layer still emits a large IDMA request despite the advertised limits.
hypotheses.upstream_note: A pass is a runtime proof and narrowing tool, not an acceptable final upstream design.

result.summary: Passed read-only root proof: EXT4 mounted ro on mmcblk0p3 and init=/bin/sh started. The run later exposed an unexpected 8-block CMD25 write that used IDMA and stalled, so the next diagnostic should identify the write issuer before enabling any write path.

hypotheses.title: Trace unexpected CMD25 write issuer before enabling writes
hypotheses.question: What kernel path issues an 8-block CMD25 write after an init=/bin/sh read-only root proof, and can it be prevented or isolated before any write-capable PIO experiment?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small printk/dump_stack patch and one boot
procedure: Do not enable multi-block PIO writes yet.
Add an A733-only diagnostic that logs and dump_stacks the first write data request, including opcode, arg, flags, blksz, blocks, sg_len, current task, and mounted root command line.
Prefer returning a controlled error for writes after logging if that preserves media safety; otherwise allow the existing stall only long enough to capture the issuer.
Boot Cubie3 once with the same PARTUUID ro root proof and capture UART.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: UART identifies the issuer/call path for the CMD25 write without completing a potentially unsafe write to the rootfs.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No stack or issuer evidence is captured before the write stalls.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This remains a lab safety diagnostic. Do not convert it into upstream policy.

result.summary: Passed as an issuer trace but intentionally blocked card init. The first write is opcode=49, task kworker/7:1, workqueue events_freezable mmc_rescan. Stack: sd_write_ext_reg -> mmc_sd_init_card -> mmc_attach_sd -> mmc_rescan. The core reports error -30 writing Cache Enable bit and card init fails. This identifies SD cache enable as the first write before any rootfs activity.

hypotheses.title: Skip SD cache-enable write and trace subsequent writes
hypotheses.question: If SD cache enable is skipped on A733, does the read-only root proof proceed and does any later write still appear?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small core diagnostic patch and one boot
procedure: Keep the A733 write guard active.
Skip only the SD cache-enable write in mmc_sd_init_card for the A733 diagnostic path.
Boot Cubie3 once with the same PARTUUID ro root proof and capture UART.
Search the UART log for diag block A733 write, opcode=25, opcode=49, EXT4 mount, and init=/bin/sh.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: The card initializes, EXT4 mounts read-only, init=/bin/sh starts, and no write request is captured in the proof window.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: A later write appears and is blocked/traced, or card init still fails.
hypotheses.upstream_note: A cache-enable skip is diagnostic. Do not treat it as final upstream policy without maintainer-facing rationale.

result.summary: Passed. Log includes 'mmc0: diag skip A733 SD cache enable', mmcblk0 p1/p2/p3, EXT4 ro mount, and init=/bin/sh. No write trace appears in the captured window.

hypotheses.title: Controlled CMD49 PIO cache-enable proof
hypotheses.question: Can the SD cache-enable CMD49 write complete through a narrowly scoped PIO write path, without enabling arbitrary block writes?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small host-driver patch and one boot
procedure: Do not enable general writes.
Add PIO write support only for opcode 49 with one 512-byte block on the A733 diagnostic path.
Keep the write guard for all other write opcodes.
Do not skip SD cache enable in the core for this test.
Boot Cubie3 once, capture whether CMD49 completes, whether the card initializes, and whether any later write is traced.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: CMD49 completes via PIO, card init proceeds, and either read-only root mounts with no later writes or the next write issuer is captured.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: CMD49 PIO fails or causes a media-risky write path outside the scoped command.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This tests write protocol mechanics only. It is not a final upstream write-path design.

result.summary: Passed. CMD49 cache-enable completed through the narrowly scoped PIO write path (`diag allow A733 CMD49 pio write`, `diag pio write opcode=49`, `diag finalize opcode=49`). The run then printed `mmcblk0: p1 p2 p3`, mounted EXT4 read-only on `mmcblk0p3`, and ran `/bin/sh` as init. No general write was enabled.

hypotheses.title: Single-descriptor IDMA CMD18 read proof
hypotheses.question: Does A733 SDMMC0 IDMA fail even for one 4096-byte CMD18 descriptor, or only when the block layer emits large multi-descriptor reads?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small host-driver patch and one boot
procedure: Keep the H008 safety rails: CMD49-only PIO write, all other writes guarded, and block-layer limits capped to 8 blocks.
For this test only, force the 8-block CMD18 path back through IDMA instead of the proven PIO read path.
Log descriptor control, descriptor size, DLBA, CHDA, CBDA, RINTR, IDST, CBCR, BBCR, and whether DATA_OVER appears.
Boot Cubie3 once with the same PARTUUID read-only root proof.
If the first 8-block IDMA CMD18 stalls before EXT4, compare the single descriptor state against the vendor baseline dump.
If it mounts, the immediate failure class is large or multi-descriptor IDMA, and the next test should increase descriptor count one step at a time.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: An 8-block single-descriptor IDMA CMD18 completes and the read-only root proof still reaches init=/bin/sh.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: The 8-block single-descriptor IDMA CMD18 stalls with IDST=0x4000 or no DATA_OVER before EXT4 can mount.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This is a runtime narrowing diagnostic only. Do not present the PIO rails or request caps as an upstream fix.

result.summary: Failed as a proof, useful as a narrowing result. The first 8-block CMD18 single-descriptor IDMA read stalls before partition parsing with RINTR=0x24, IDST=0x4000, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x400, and BBCR=0. The immediate blocker is descriptor-read progress even for one descriptor, not only large multi-descriptor block I/O.

hypotheses.title: Single-descriptor IDMA vendor comparison
hypotheses.question: Which descriptor, DMA address, or visible IDMAC state differs between the H009 single-descriptor mainline stall and the vendor working-read baseline?
hypotheses.test_cost: low; no boot before source/log comparison
procedure: Use task-packets/kernel/a733-sdmmc-single-desc-comparison-20260610T1612Z.json as the starting evidence table.

Compare H009 against the vendor wide sample for CMDR, RINTR, STAS, THLD, DMAC, DLBA, IDST, IDIE, CHDA, CBDA, CBCR, BBCR, DRV_DL, SAMP_DL, SMCV, descriptor control, descriptor size, descriptor list address, and data-buffer address.
Inspect the vendor v5p3x descriptor setup and DMA mask/allocation path only for differences that explain a one-descriptor descriptor-read stall.
Do not build another boot variant unless the comparison identifies a concrete source-backed delta.
If a concrete delta is found, create the next minimal diagnostic patch from that one delta only.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A concrete source-backed difference is identified that can explain why vendor IDMAC advances CHDA/CBDA while mainline remains at CHDA=DLBA and CBDA=0.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No descriptor, address, or visible IDMAC setup difference remains; next work must move to hidden wrapper, coherency, or bus-fabric behavior with new evidence.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This is evidence shaping only. It does not alter maintainer-facing DTS scope.

result.summary: Vendor live descriptor memory for a 4 KiB read uses the same practical shape as H009/H011: des0 0x8000001c or 0x0000001c, size 0x00001000, shifted data-buffer address, and shifted next-descriptor address. The descriptor geometry hypothesis is closed for now. The remaining visible clue is a DMAC/readback discrepancy around bit 0x200, but H011 proves a direct REG_DMAC clear is not the fix.

hypotheses.title: A733 IDMAC control clear diagnostic
hypotheses.question: Does clearing REG_DMAC before enabling IDMAC remove mainline's extra DMAC bit 0x200 and let a one-descriptor CMD18 transfer advance?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; one host-driver patch, one build, one boot
procedure: Keep the H009 safety rails: CMD49-only PIO write, all other writes guarded, and a forced one-descriptor CMD18 IDMA read.
After the vendor-style DMA reset sequence, write REG_DMAC=0 before setting SDXC_DMA_ENABLE_BIT.
Log the immediate REG_DMAC readback before enable.
Boot Cubie3 with the corrected-root proof and capture descriptor plus IDMAC register state.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: REG_DMAC readback loses bit 0x200 and the one-descriptor CMD18 read advances beyond descriptor-read state.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: REG_DMAC still reads with bit 0x200 set, or IDST remains 0x4000 with CHDA=DLBA and CBDA=0.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This is lab-only source narrowing. Do not present direct DMAC clearing as an upstream fix.

result.summary: Failed. REG_DMAC=0 immediately reads back as 0x00000200 before enable; post-command state is still DMAC=0x282, RINTR=0x24, IDST=0x4000, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x400, and BBCR=0.

hypotheses.title: Identify source of sticky DMAC bit 0x200
hypotheses.question: Is mainline's sticky DMAC bit 0x200 a causal A733 IDMAC setup difference, a read-only/derived status bit, or a non-causal artifact?
hypotheses.test_cost: low first; source audit plus one vendor live register sample before any build
procedure: Collect one vendor live 4 KiB read sample that includes GCTRL as well as DMAC, DLBA, IDST, CHDA, CBDA, CBCR, BBCR, and descriptor memory in the fixed register-dump schema.
Audit the vendor v5p3x SDMMC driver, mainline sunxi-mmc definitions, and available manuals for REG_DMAC bit 9 semantics or adjacent wrapper state.
If bit 0x200 is read-only, derived from GCTRL/reset, or otherwise non-causal, close the DMAC clue and move to hidden wrapper/coherency/fabric evidence.
Only if the audit finds a concrete source-backed control delta, build one minimal diagnostic patch that changes that delta alone.
After any boot, restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733 and update this queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: The source/meaning of bit 0x200 is identified strongly enough to justify one minimal next diagnostic or to discard it as non-causal.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No source-backed meaning is found; next work must stop treating DMAC bit 0x200 as actionable and move to wrapper/coherency/fabric evidence collection.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This remains private lab evidence. It does not expand the maintainer-facing DTS series or duplicate the in-flight CCU/pinctrl RFCs.

result.summary: Completed. Vendor working 4 KiB reads also show DMAC 0x00000200 and 0x00000282, so bit 0x200 is not a mainline-only setup clue. Mainline source defines bit 9 for IDST abnormal interrupt, but has no REG_DMAC bit-9 definition. The source-backed conclusion is that DMAC bit 0x200 should be treated as non-causal unless a later manual/BSP source names it. The vendor live sample instead exposes GCTRL=0x20000830 during working reads, while H013 proves the cleaned mainline path uses GCTRL=0x20000030 and still stalls.

hypotheses.title: Clean PIO-to-IDMA DMA access mode
hypotheses.question: Does clearing the lab PIO rail's leftover SDXC_ACCESS_BY_AHB bit before IDMA let the one-descriptor CMD18 transfer advance?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; one host-driver patch, one build, one boot
procedure: Remove the H011 direct REG_DMAC=0 diagnostic.
Before enabling IDMA on A733, clear SDXC_ACCESS_BY_AHB in GCTRL and log the readback.
Boot Cubie3 with the same corrected-root direct ext4load proof.
Compare GCTRL, DMAC, IDST, CHDA, CBDA, CBCR, and BBCR against H009/H011 and the H012 vendor sample.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: The one-descriptor CMD18 IDMA transfer advances beyond descriptor-read state or reaches partition parsing.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: GCTRL no longer has ACCESS_BY_AHB set but IDST remains 0x4000 with CHDA=DLBA and CBDA=0.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This is lab-only cleanup of a previous diagnostic side effect, not a DTS or upstream driver proposal.

result.summary: Failed. The diagnostic changes DMA launch to GCTRL=0x20000030, matching DMA access mode instead of the contaminated H009/H011 GCTRL=0xa0000030. The transfer still stalls at RINTR=0x24, IDST=0x4000, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x400, and BBCR=0.

hypotheses.title: Identify vendor GCTRL bit 0x00000800
hypotheses.question: What does the vendor working-read GCTRL bit 0x00000800 mean, and is it a source-backed control delta for A733 IDMAC descriptor progress?
hypotheses.test_cost: low first; source/manual audit before build
procedure: Search the available vendor BSP, U-Boot, manuals, and register definitions for SDMMC GCTRL bit 11 or literal 0x00000800 use on v5p3x/A733.
Compare H012 vendor GCTRL=0x20000830 against H013 mainline GCTRL=0x20000030 after ACCESS_BY_AHB is cleared.
If bit 0x00000800 is identified as a control bit plausibly required for IDMA, build one minimal diagnostic that sets only that bit before IDMA.
If no source-backed meaning is found, do not guess-test it; move to hidden wrapper, coherency, or storage-fabric evidence collection.
After any boot, restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733 and update this queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A source-backed meaning for GCTRL bit 0x00000800 is found and it either justifies one minimal diagnostic patch or is ruled out.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No source-backed meaning is found; the next queue item must move away from visible GCTRL/DMAC bits.
hypotheses.upstream_note: Do not add undocumented magic GCTRL bits to upstream code. This is private lab triage only unless backed by a public datasheet/BSP explanation.

result.summary: Completed without build. Vendor source names bit 11 as SDXC_DTIME_UNIT and uses it only to scale read-data timeout in the v5p3x manual RDTO path. Vendor runtime confirms the bit pairs with TMOUT=0x01312dff during reads and clears with TMOUT=0xffffffff afterward. Do not guess-test this bit for descriptor-fetch progress.

hypotheses.title: Audit hidden DMA coherency and storage-fabric attributes
hypotheses.question: What hidden wrapper, DMA coherency, MBUS/storage-fabric, or DMA translation difference remains after descriptor geometry, visible DMAC, and visible GCTRL bits are closed?
hypotheses.test_cost: low first; source/log audit before build
procedure: Compare vendor BSP descriptor allocation, dma_map_sg path, DMA mask, coherent_dma_mask, des_addr_shift, descriptor sync, cache maintenance, and any MBUS/storage fabric setup against the current diagnostic branch.

Use existing failed diagnostics to mark already-falsified variants: 64-bit DMA mask, descriptor dma_sync_single_for_device, vendor descriptor geometry, request page count, MSI-lite clock, and visible GCTRL/DMAC bits.
If one concrete source-backed coherency/fabric delta remains, create one minimal diagnostic patch for that delta only.
If no concrete delta remains, collect one new evidence sample that can distinguish whether IDMAC cannot fetch the descriptor or fetches stale descriptor contents.
After any boot, restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733 and update this queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A source-backed hidden coherency/fabric delta is identified and either tested or queued with a precise proof plan.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No source-backed delta remains; the next queue item must gather new hardware evidence rather than cycling source-visible guesses.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This is private lab triage. Do not expand the upstream DTS slice or add undocumented fabric magic without source-backed evidence.

result.summary: Completed without build. Vendor and current diagnostic source now match or already cover the cheap hidden-DMA/fabric candidates: descriptor geometry, shifted descriptor/data addresses, 64-bit DMA mask, coherent descriptor allocation, descriptor sync, sg_len=dma_len=1, explicit storage fabric clocks, MSI-lite mapping, and descriptor page count. H013 post-stall evidence leaves descriptor memory unchanged with OWN set while CHDA=DLBA and CBDA=0, so the next step is a hardware descriptor-fetch reachability sample rather than another source-visible control guess.

hypotheses.title: Descriptor-fetch reachability stamp proof
hypotheses.question: Can A733 IDMAC fetch any coherent descriptor contents from the programmed DLBA, or is the descriptor read path blocked before it consumes descriptor word 0?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; one instrumentation-only kernel patch and one Cubie3 boot
procedure: Keep the H013 safe rails: corrected DMA access mode, CMD49-only PIO write, read-only root proof, and one 8-block CMD18 forced through IDMA.

Add an A733-only instrumentation patch that writes distinctive nonfunctional stamp values into unused descriptor words and adjacent descriptors before IDMA start, without changing descriptor control semantics.
Immediately before REG_CMDR, log DLBA, sg_dma, descriptor physical address, descriptor words 0-3 for descriptor 0, descriptor 1 stamp words, and a short checksum of the coherent descriptor ring.

After the existing post-data poll window, call dma_sync_single_for_cpu for the coherent descriptor ring only for diagnostics, then log the same descriptor words/checksum plus CHDA, CBDA, IDST, CBCR, and BBCR.

Boot Cubie3 once from Strix over UART and capture the log. The automation must verify the real U-Boot => prompt or send Ctrl-C out of the extlinux menu before issuing setenv/ext4load/booti. Do not submit or rewrite upstream patches.

Interpretation rule: if descriptor word 0 OWN clears, CHDA advances, CBDA becomes the buffer address, or stamped adjacent descriptors are reflected in controller progress, IDMAC fetched descriptor memory and the blocker is later in data movement. If descriptor words remain unchanged, OWN stays set, CHDA remains DLBA, and CBDA remains zero, treat descriptor-fetch reachability or address translation as the next blocker.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, update this queue, update the private KB, update docs/status.md, commit, sync to Strix, and push or record blocked remotes.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: The run proves IDMAC fetched descriptor memory or advanced beyond DLBA, narrowing the next test to data buffer movement.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: The run repeats unchanged descriptor memory, OWN set, CHDA=DLBA, and CBDA=0 after a CPU-side coherent sync, narrowing the next test to descriptor-fetch reachability/address translation.
hypotheses.upstream_note: Instrumentation only. Do not convert descriptor stamps, lab rails, or printk tracing into upstream code.

attempts.summary: UART automation missed the real U-Boot prompt and sent direct U-Boot commands into the extlinux menu. No H016 kernel boot occurred. Cubie3 was power-cycled and restored to vendor 5.15.147-21-a733. Rerun H016 with prompt-controlled automation that reaches => or escapes extlinux with Ctrl-C before ext4load/booti.

Prompt-controlled automation reached the real U-Boot prompt, loaded the H016 Image/DTB by ext4load, and booted Linux 7.1.0-rc5-00274-g529f1682dd48. The log proves SDXC detection and mmcblk0 creation before the forced CMD18 IDMA read. Descriptor checksum/stamps were unchanged after dma_sync_single_for_cpu, OWN stayed set, CHDA stayed at DLBA, CBDA stayed zero, and IDST stayed 0x4000. Cubie3 was restored to vendor 5.15.147-21-a733.

result.summary: Failed as a boot proof but succeeded as a narrowing proof. The controller reaches CMD18 with RINTR=0x24 and IDST=0x4000, but the coherent descriptor ring remains byte-for-byte unchanged after the stall. Treat the next blocker as descriptor-fetch reachability, DMA address translation, or hidden fabric visibility before descriptor word 0 is consumed.

hypotheses.title: Descriptor-fetch address translation and fabric comparison
hypotheses.question: Which source-backed address-translation or fabric-visibility difference could prevent A733 SDMMC0 IDMAC from fetching the coherent descriptor at DLBA?
hypotheses.test_cost: low first; source and log audit before any build
procedure: Do not build a new kernel until this no-build comparison identifies one concrete control delta.
Compare H016 DLBA, descriptor CPU-visible words, descriptor DMA address, sg DMA address, dma mask, coherent allocation path, and SDMMC0 fabric clocks against the vendor 4 KiB working-read samples.

Audit vendor A733 SDMMC, CCU/PRCM, MBUS, IOMMU, MSI-lite, DMA mask, and any storage-fabric init paths for descriptor-list address translation or master-access enable behavior that is not already covered by H015.
Fill the fixed register/evidence schema with address, vendor value at time T, mainline value at time T, delta, interpretation, and source reference.
If exactly one concrete source-backed delta remains, build a minimal diagnostic patch for that one delta only and boot Cubie3 once from Strix.
If no concrete source-backed delta remains, queue a lower-level proof that changes the descriptor allocation/access path rather than guessing more visible SDMMC registers.
After any boot, restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, update this queue, update the private KB, update docs/status.md, commit, sync to Strix, and push or record blocked remotes.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A concrete source-backed descriptor-fetch/address/fabric delta is found and either tested or queued with a single-change proof plan.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No source-backed delta remains; the next runtime test must change the descriptor memory placement/access path rather than visible SDMMC control bits.
hypotheses.upstream_note: Private lab triage only. Do not expand the maintainer-facing DTS slice, add vendor U-Boot properties, or duplicate the in-flight A733 CCU/pinctrl RFCs.

result.summary: Completed without a kernel build. The no-build audit found no remaining source-backed IOMMU, descriptor geometry, descriptor address shift, 64-bit DMA-mask, req-page-count, or SDMMC0 storage-fabric clock delta. Vendor working reads and H016 both use shifted descriptor/data addresses, 4 KiB descriptor segments, coherent descriptor rings below 4 GiB, and above-4G data buffers. H016 still proves descriptor word 0 is not consumed. Queue H018 as a descriptor-placement/access-path proof, not another visible SDMMC register guess.

hypotheses.title: Descriptor-stamp replay without forced 64-bit DMA mask
hypotheses.question: Does A733 IDMAC fail to fetch the descriptor when H016 stamps are placed in the default/pre-64-bit-mask coherent descriptor window?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; one tiny diagnostic patch and one Cubie3 boot
procedure: Start on Strix from /srv/projects/a733-diag-rtc-extosc-orphan at H016 commit 529f1682dd48 or an equivalent branch containing the H016 descriptor-stamp instrumentation.
Create a new diagnostic branch and remove only the A733 64-bit dma_set_mask_and_coherent diagnostic change before descriptor allocation.
Keep H016 unchanged otherwise: descriptor stamps, before/after checksum logs, CMD49 PIO safety rail, read-only root proof, and forced one-descriptor 8-block CMD18 IDMA.
Build the kernel/Image/DTB and boot Cubie3 once from Strix using prompt-controlled UART automation that reaches the real U-Boot => prompt before ext4load/booti.
Capture sg_dma, DLBA, descriptor words, checksum/stamps, CHDA, CBDA, IDST, CBCR, and BBCR before command launch and after the stall.
If the descriptor ring remains unchanged and CHDA=DLBA with CBDA=0, close descriptor placement as the cause and move to SDMMC0 master/fabric reachability below visible registers.
If IDMAC advances or consumes descriptor word 0, treat the forced 64-bit allocation/high-low descriptor placement as the primary suspect.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, update this queue, update the private KB, update docs/status.md, commit, sync to Strix, and push or record blocked remotes.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: IDMAC advances, clears OWN, updates CHDA/CBDA, or otherwise consumes descriptor memory under the default/pre-64-bit descriptor placement.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: Descriptor checksum/stamps remain unchanged, OWN remains set, CHDA remains DLBA, and CBDA remains zero under the default/pre-64-bit descriptor placement.
hypotheses.upstream_note: Instrumentation only. Do not submit this diagnostic or convert it into an upstream DMA-mask policy without a minimal source-backed fix.

result.summary: Completed and failed as expected. Removing the forced A733 64-bit DMA-mask diagnostic moved the data buffer below 4 GiB, but the descriptor ring stayed at sg_dma=0xfe100000/DLBA=0x3f840000. The forced one-descriptor CMD18 still stalled with unchanged descriptor checksum/stamps, OWN set, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, and IDST=0x4000. H018 closes data-buffer >4G placement and the forced DMA-mask path as causes, but not descriptor allocation class.

hypotheses.title: Streaming-mapped descriptor ring outside coherent CMA
hypotheses.question: Can A733 IDMAC fetch descriptors from a normal streaming-mapped descriptor ring instead of the coherent allocation at 0xfe100000?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; one descriptor-allocation diagnostic patch and one Cubie3 boot
procedure: Start on Strix from H018 commit 7adf8f1abf95 or an equivalent branch containing the descriptor stamps and no forced A733 64-bit DMA-mask call.

Change only the descriptor ring allocation/access path for A733: allocate the same 8192-byte ring from normal zeroed memory, map it with dma_map_single or equivalent for DMA_BIDIRECTIONAL, and keep host->sg_dma as the mapped DMA address.
Keep explicit dma_sync_single_for_device before command launch and dma_sync_single_for_cpu after the poll window so descriptor stamps/checksum remain comparable to H016/H018.
Keep all H016/H018 rails: CMD49 PIO write, read-only root proof, forced one-descriptor 8-block CMD18, descriptor stamps, and before/after descriptor dumps.
Build, stage to Cubie3, boot once from Strix using prompt-controlled U-Boot ext4load/booti, capture UART, and restore Cubie3 to vendor 5.15.147-21-a733.
If IDMAC consumes descriptor word 0 or advances CHDA/CBDA, treat coherent/CMA descriptor visibility as the suspect.
If descriptor checksum/stamps remain unchanged with OWN set, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, and IDST=0x4000, move below descriptor memory class toward SDMMC0 master/fabric reachability.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: IDMAC advances, clears OWN, updates CHDA/CBDA, or otherwise consumes descriptor memory from the streaming-mapped ring.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: Streaming-mapped descriptor memory remains unchanged after the stall, with OWN set, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, and IDST=0x4000.
hypotheses.upstream_note: Instrumentation only. Do not submit a streaming-descriptor rewrite; use it only to decide whether the coherent descriptor allocation path is involved.

result.summary: Completed and failed as expected. The descriptor ring moved out of coherent CMA into streaming-mapped normal memory at sg_dma=0xfa800000/DLBA=0x3ea00000, and the data buffer landed at sg0 dma=0xfa802000. The forced CMD18 still stalled with descriptor checksum/stamps unchanged after dma_sync_single_for_cpu, OWN set, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, IDST=0x4000, CBCR=0x400, and BBCR=0. H019 closes descriptor allocation class as the immediate cause.

hypotheses.title: Vendor/mainline non-SDMMC fabric snapshot
hypotheses.question: Which non-SDMMC register or security/fabric state differs when vendor SDMMC0 IDMAC can fetch descriptors but mainline cannot fetch descriptor word 0?
hypotheses.test_cost: low first; source-guided register inventory and vendor/mainline snapshot before any behavior patch
procedure: Do not guess-test another SDMMC-visible control bit.

Build a source-backed register inventory for only SDMMC0 descriptor-fetch reachability: CCU/PRCM gates and resets for SDMMC0, MBUS/store/MSI-lite fabric clocks, any A733 storage/firewall/security registers named by vendor source, and the SDMMC0 register window already in the fixed schema.

On vendor kernel, collect a live Cubie3 snapshot before and after one small SD read, preferably `dd if=/dev/mmcblk0 of=/dev/null bs=4K count=1 iflag=direct`, using devmem or the existing vendor dump tool from Strix over SSH.
On the latest mainline diagnostic, collect the same register set at dma-entry, idma-post-cmdr, and post-stall, reusing UART printk if a build is necessary only for observability.
Fill a fixed comparison schema with address, register name, vendor value, mainline value, delta, source reference, and interpretation.
Only if one concrete non-SDMMC delta remains, queue exactly one minimal H021 behavior patch for that delta.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, update this queue, update the private KB, update docs/status.md, commit, sync to Strix, and push or record blocked remotes.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A concrete, source-backed non-SDMMC fabric/security/reset/clock delta is found and narrowed enough for one H021 patch.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No source-backed non-SDMMC delta is found; the next step must move to external evidence such as vendor driver tracing, bus/firewall documentation, or maintainer/RFC coordination rather than another local behavior guess.
hypotheses.upstream_note: Private lab evidence only. Do not add undocumented fabric magic, vendor U-Boot policy, or out-of-scope peripherals to the upstream DTS slice.

hypotheses.title: Retest descriptor geometry, descriptor size, address shifting, DIC, chain bits, CEATA, DMAC reset, fabric-clock order, or vendor delay
hypotheses.question: Previously tested visible descriptor/control variants
hypotheses.test_cost: wasteful unless new source evidence changes the expected value
procedure: Do not rerun these variants as exploratory guesses.
Only reopen a subcase if H001/H002 identifies a concrete mismatch or missing vendor behavior.
fail_history: v5p3x 4 KiB descriptor segmentation still stalls.
DIC and chain-bit variants still stall.
CEATA omission and DMAC reset variants still stall.
Vendor-like DRV_DL does not fix CMD18.
Explicit descriptor dma_sync_single_for_device does not fix CMD18.
Preserving vendor/bootloader SAMP_DL=0x00002000 and skipping mainline sample calibration does not fix CMD18.
Using the vendor SDMMC0 MSI Lite1 clock gate via CLK_BUS_MSI_LITE1 does not fix CMD18.
